name: Somaliland

colors.symbolism: Islam — and the upper stripe bears the Shahada
the blood of those who died for independence
the star and the Shahada inscription

description: Three horizontal stripes — green, white, red — with the Arabic inscription of the Islamic shahada in black on the green stripe and a black five-pointed star centred on the white stripe.
history: Flag of the de-facto independent Republic of Somaliland, declared on 18 May 1991 after the collapse of the Siad Barre government in Somalia. Adopted on 14 October 1996. Although Somaliland operates as a sovereign state with its own government, currency, and military, it has not received international recognition.

features.role: Arabic shahada — 'There is no god but Allah; Muhammad is the messenger of Allah'
